page.primary_keyword: estepona marina vs puerto banus departure
page.title: Estepona Marina vs Puerto Banús: Best Departure Point

data.meta_description: Estepona Marina vs Puerto Banús: compare departure points for your Marbella boat charter. Distances, facilities, and best boats for each base.
data.summary: Compare Estepona Marina and Puerto Banús as departure points for your boat charter in Marbella. This guide covers distances, facilities, and which boats are available from each base.
key_facts: Puerto Banús is 6 NM from Marbella; Estepona Marina is 8 NM from Marbella.
Puerto Banús offers luxury dining, shopping, and nightlife; Estepona is quieter and more traditional.
All our boats depart from Puerto Banús; Estepona charters require a repositioning fee.
Puerto Banús has 24-hour port control and fuel dock; Estepona has limited services.
Estepona Marina is closer to Sotogrande (12 NM) and Gibraltar (20 NM).
Puerto Banús is the most popular departure point for Marbella boat rentals.
Estepona Marina is better for a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere.

faq.q: Which marina is bigger: Estepona or Puerto Banús?
faq.a: Puerto Banús is larger with over 900 berths, while Estepona Marina has around 400. Puerto Banús also has more extensive facilities including restaurants, shops, and a heliport.

faq.q: Can I charter a boat from Estepona Marina?
faq.a: Yes, but all our boats are based in Puerto Banús. For Estepona departures, a repositioning fee applies. Contact us via WhatsApp for a quote.

faq.q: Is Estepona Marina cheaper than Puerto Banús for parking?
faq.a: Yes, parking in Estepona is generally cheaper and easier. Puerto Banús parking can cost €5-10 per hour or more during peak season.

faq.q: Which departure point is best for a sunset cruise?
faq.a: Puerto Banús offers iconic sunset views over the marina and the Golden Mile. Estepona provides a more tranquil setting with views of the Sierra Bermeja mountains.

faq.q: How far is Estepona from Puerto Banús by sea?
faq.a: Estepona Marina is approximately 8 nautical miles west of Puerto Banús. By boat, it takes about 20-30 minutes depending on speed and conditions.

faq.q: Are there any boat tours from Estepona Marina?
faq.a: Yes, some local operators offer boat tours from Estepona, but our fleet is exclusively based in Puerto Banús. We can arrange a transfer or meet you at Estepona with prior arrangement.
Puerto Banús has a wider range of upscale restaurants, including Michelin-starred options. Estepona has excellent seafood restaurants but with less variety.

faq.q: Can I walk to the beach from either marina?
faq.a: Both marinas have nearby beaches. Puerto Banús has Levante Beach and the Golden Mile. Estepona Marina is adjacent to the town's main beach, Playa de la Rada.

data.body_html:
Choosing between Estepona Marina and Puerto Banús as your departure point for a boat charter in Marbella can shape your entire day on the water. Both marinas offer unique advantages, but they cater to different preferences. This guide breaks down the key differences to help you decide which base is best for your charter.

Distance and Location

Puerto Banús is located just 6 nautical miles west of Marbella town centre, making it the most convenient departure point for exploring the Marbella coastline, including the Golden Mile and Cabopino. Estepona Marina is 8 NM west of Marbella, closer to Sotogrande (12 NM) and Gibraltar (20 NM). If you want to head east towards Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, Puerto Banús is the better choice. For westbound trips to Estepona or beyond, Estepona saves you travel time.

Facilities and Atmosphere

Puerto Banús is world-famous for its luxury yachts, designer boutiques, and vibrant nightlife. The marina has 24-hour port control, fuel docks, and dozens of restaurants and bars. Estepona Marina is smaller and more relaxed, with a traditional Andalusian feel. It offers essential services like water, electricity, and a fuel dock, but lacks the high-end amenities of Puerto Banús. If you want a lively atmosphere before or after your charter, Puerto Banús is ideal. For a quieter, family-friendly vibe, choose Estepona.

Our Fleet and Departure Points

All our boats, including the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, Mangusta 80, and Ferretti 94, are based in Puerto Banús. This means that for Estepona departures, we need to reposition the boat, which incurs an additional fee. Contact us via WhatsApp for a personalised quote if you prefer to depart from Estepona. The most popular option is to start from Puerto Banús and include a stop at Estepona during your charter.

Best Boats for Each Departure

For a day trip from Puerto Banús, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) is perfect for cruising the Marbella coastline. If you want to visit Estepona, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers speed and comfort for the 8 NM journey. For a budget-friendly option from Puerto Banús, the Bandido (11.5m, 8 pax) is a great choice. The larger Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) is ideal for extended trips to Sotogrande or Gibraltar.

Parking and Access

Parking at Puerto Banús can be expensive, especially during summer, with rates up to €10 per hour. Estepona Marina offers cheaper parking, often free or €1-2 per hour. Both marinas are easily accessible by car and taxi from Marbella and nearby towns. Puerto Banús is also well-served by public transport. If you're driving, Estepona is more budget-friendly for parking.

What to Expect on Your Charter

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. The price includes Spanish IVA (21%). For example, the Astondoa 40 costs €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for a full day (8 hours). The Mangusta 80 starts at €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. All prices are all-inclusive.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which marina is bigger: Estepona or Puerto Banús?

Puerto Banús is larger with over 900 berths, while Estepona Marina has around 400. Puerto Banús also has more extensive facilities including restaurants, shops, and a heliport.

Can I charter a boat from Estepona Marina?

Yes, but all our boats are based in Puerto Banús. For Estepona departures, a repositioning fee applies. Contact us via WhatsApp for a quote.

Is Estepona Marina cheaper than Puerto Banús for parking?

Yes, parking in Estepona is generally cheaper and easier. Puerto Banús parking can cost €5-10 per hour or more during peak season.

Which departure point is best for a sunset cruise?

Puerto Banús offers iconic sunset views over the marina and the Golden Mile. Estepona provides a more tranquil setting with views of the Sierra Bermeja mountains.

How far is Estepona from Puerto Banús by sea?

Estepona Marina is approximately 8 nautical miles west of Puerto Banús. By boat, it takes about 20-30 minutes depending on speed and conditions.

Are there any boat tours from Estepona Marina?

Yes, some local operators offer boat tours from Estepona, but our fleet is exclusively based in Puerto Banús. We can arrange a transfer or meet you at Estepona with prior arrangement.

Which marina has better restaurants?

Puerto Banús has a wider range of upscale restaurants, including Michelin-starred options. Estepona has excellent seafood restaurants but with less variety.

Can I walk to the beach from either marina?

Both marinas have nearby beaches. Puerto Banús has Levante Beach and the Golden Mile. Estepona Marina is adjacent to the town's main beach, Playa de la Rada.

Ready to book your charter? Browse our fleet and choose your departure point. For Estepona departures, contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote.
